name: Aomori Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity and the snows of the north
the forests and natural abundance of Aomori

description: A white field charged at the centre with a green stylised map of Aomori Prefecture, showing the characteristic two peninsulas around Mutsu Bay.
history: Aomori's prefectural symbol was selected from a public design competition and adopted by prefectural notice on 16 August 1961, with the flag adopted alongside it. The mark is a stylised outline of the prefecture itself.

features.symbolism: the geographic shape of Aomori, with its two peninsulas (Tsugaru and Shimokita) embracing Mutsu Bay; symbolises the prefecture's growth and development.
